metadata.name: ww-chat
metadata.description: **Renders entirely from data — must be seeded with at least one current-user participant + 2-3 demo messages and given a `min-height` (500px) to be visible.** A full-featured chat UI with header, scrolling messages area, and input with attachments. Messages and participants are two separate data sources linked by IDs: each `messages[].senderId` must match one `participants[].id`. The chat auto-renders from that data and does NOT accept child elements (no slots). Built-in support for group chats, date separators, read-only mode, attachment uploads, 40+ locales, and formula-based field mapping for custom backend shapes.
features: Header with name/avatar/location/status (auto-switches to a group label when 2+ participants)
Messages list with auto-scroll, date separators, and attachment thumbnails
Input area: textarea + send button + optional attachment button
Keyboard: `Enter` sends the message, `Shift+Enter` inserts a newline
Participants model with `isCurrentUser` flag — drives own-message vs other-message styling
Attachments: uploaded files become `pendingAttachments` until send; sent attachments appear in `messages[].attachments`
Formula-based field mappings for custom message/participant/attachment shapes (optional — defaults match the standard shape)
40+ locales for date/time formatting via date-fns
Read-only `chatState` variable exposing the fully resolved state
LocalContext `chat` for children to read messages, conversation metadata, current user
Events: messageSent, messageReceived, messageRightClick, attachmentClick, pendingAttachmentClick, close

properties.name: messages
participants
properties.description:
Array of participants. Default shape: `{ id, name, avatar, location, status, isCurrentUser? }`. Status is one of `online | offline | away | busy`. Exactly ONE participant should have `isCurrentUser: true` — this is the local user whose messages are aligned right. If none is marked, every message is styled as 'other'. Every `senderId` in `messages` should match a participant `id`. Example participant:
```json
{
  "id": "user-1",
  "name": "Alice",
  "avatar": "https://i.pravatar.cc/80?u=alice",
  "location": "Paris, FR",
  "status": "online",
  "isCurrentUser": false
}
```

properties.name: groupChatText
properties.description: Custom header text for group chats (2+ participants). If empty, falls back to the auto-generated `Group Chat (X participants)` label.

properties.name: groupChatAvatar
properties.description: Image URL used as the group avatar when there are 2+ participants. Falls back to auto-generated initials if empty.

properties.name: allowAttachments
properties.description: Show a paperclip button in the input that lets users attach files. When false, `pendingAttachmentClick` never fires and the `messageSent` payload will have an empty `attachments` array.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disable the input area entirely (textarea + send + attachment buttons). Incoming messages still render and auto-scroll continues to work.

properties.name: autoScrollBehavior
properties.description: Scroll animation when new messages arrive or `scrollToBottom` fires.

properties.name: emptyMessageText
properties.description: Placeholder text shown in the messages area when `messages` is empty.

properties.name: headerShowCloseButton
properties.description: Show a close (×) button in the header. When clicked, it fires the `close` event — you're responsible for hiding / unmounting the chat in response.

properties.name: locale
properties.description: Locale used for date/time formatting (date-fns locale code). Common values: `enUS`, `enGB`, `fr`, `de`, `es`, `it`, `pt`, `ptBR`, `ja`, `zh`, `ko`, `ar`, `hi`, `nl`, `sv`, `tr`, `pl`. 40+ locales available — full list: https://date-fns.org/v2/docs/I18n.

properties.name: timeFormat
properties.description: Format for displayed times using a date-fns pattern. Common values: `h:mm a` (12h, e.g. `9:05 PM`), `HH:mm` (24h, e.g. `21:05`), `h:mm:ss a` (with seconds).

properties.name: todayText
properties.description: Label used in the date separator for today's messages.

properties.name: yesterdayText
properties.description: Label used in the date separator for yesterday's messages.

properties.name: justNowText
properties.description: Label used for messages sent within the last minute.

properties.name: inputPlaceholder
properties.description: Placeholder text shown in the composer textarea when empty (e.g. `Type a message...`, `Say something...`).

properties.name: inputActionAlign
properties.description: Vertical alignment of the action buttons (send, attachment) relative to the multi-line textarea.

properties.name: sendIcon
properties.description: Icon for the send button. System-icon name (e.g. `send`, `chevron-right`, `arrow-up`).

properties.name: attachmentIcon
properties.description: Icon for the attachment button. System-icon name (e.g. `paperclip`, `plus`, `image`). Only visible when `allowAttachments` is true.

properties.name: removeIcon
properties.description: Icon shown on pending attachments to remove them before sending. System-icon name (e.g. `x`, `trash`).

properties.name: fontFamily
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — font family applied to the entire chat (header, messages, input). CSS font-family string, e.g. `Inter, sans-serif` or `JetBrains Mono, monospace`. Required to propagate the page's typography into the chat's internal sub-components.

properties.name: headerBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the chat header bar (name, avatar, status line).

properties.name: headerTextColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color in the header (name, location, participant count).

properties.name: headerBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border shorthand for the bottom edge of the header, e.g. `1px solid #00FF41`.

properties.name: groupChatAvatarColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the group chat avatar initials circle.

properties.name: messagesAreaBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the scrolling messages area (between header and input).

properties.name: emptyMessageColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color for the empty-state label shown when `messages` is empty.

properties.name: dateSeparatorTextColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color of the date separator labels (Today / Yesterday / dates). Targets the DOM element with class `ww-message-list__date-separator`. This is the ONLY way to set this color — custom CSS classes or selectors will NOT override it.

properties.name: dateSeparatorLineColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — color of the horizontal line flanking each date separator (DOM class `ww-message-list__date-separator`). The ONLY way to set this color — custom CSS will NOT override it.

properties.name: dateSeparatorBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the centered date-separator PILL (DOM class `ww-message-list__date-separator`). CRITICAL: left at default (white) on a dark page produces a visible white pill floating in your dark chat. Always set to match `messagesAreaBgColor` or a darker variant. The ONLY way to set this background — custom CSS will NOT override it.

properties.name: dateSeparatorBorderRadius
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — border-radius of the date-separator pill (DOM class `ww-message-list__date-separator`), e.g. `4px`, `999px`. The ONLY way to set this radius — custom CSS will NOT override it.

properties.name: messageBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of OTHER participants' message bubbles (left side).

properties.name: messageTextColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color of OTHER participants' messages.

properties.name: messageBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border shorthand for OTHER messages' bubbles (e.g. `1px solid rgba(0,255,65,0.3)`).

properties.name: messageRadius
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — border-radius of OTHER messages' bubbles (e.g. `12px`, `2px`, `16px 16px 16px 4px`).

properties.name: ownMessageBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the CURRENT user's message bubbles (right side). Usually the accent/primary color.

properties.name: ownMessageTextColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color of the current user's messages.

properties.name: ownMessageBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border shorthand for current-user bubbles.

properties.name: ownMessageRadius
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — border-radius of current-user bubbles.

properties.name: inputBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the input area (wraps the textarea + send button).

properties.name: inputTextColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color inside the textarea composer.

properties.name: inputPlaceholderColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — text color of the input placeholder when empty.

properties.name: inputAreaBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border shorthand at the top of the input area (separator from messages list).

properties.name: textareaBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border around the textarea element itself.

properties.name: textareaBorderFocus
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border around the textarea when focused.

properties.name: inputBorderRadius
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — border-radius of the textarea composer.

properties.name: sendButtonBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the send button.

properties.name: sendButtonHoverBgColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — background color of the send button on hover.

properties.name: sendButtonBorder
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS border shorthand for the send button (e.g. `1px solid #00FF41`).

properties.name: sendButtonBorderRadius
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — border-radius of the send button (e.g. `4px` for flat/square, `999px` for circular).

properties.name: sendButtonBoxShadow
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — CSS box-shadow on the send button. Set to `none` to flatten the default soft blue shadow in non-default themes.

properties.name: sendIconColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — icon color inside the send button.

properties.name: attachmentIconColor
properties.description: THEME TOKEN — icon color inside the attachment button.

events.name: messageSent
events.description: Fires when the current user sends a message (Enter or send button). The message is NOT auto-appended to `messages` — handle it yourself (push to the array, POST to your API, etc.). The payload's `senderId` will be the `id` of the participant marked `isCurrentUser: true`.

message.senderId: string (matches participants[].id of the current user)
message.attachments: array (File objects if allowAttachments was true)

events.name: messageReceived
events.description: Fires when a new message appears in `messages` with a `senderId` that is NOT the current user. Inferred from array changes — does NOT fire on initial load or when the current user sends. Useful to play a sound / badge an unread count.

events.name: messageRightClick
events.description: Fires on right-click (or long-press on touch) of a message. Use for context menus (Copy, Delete, React). Includes coordinates relative to the chat element (for positioning an in-chat menu) and relative to the viewport (for a fixed-position menu).

events.name: attachmentClick
events.description: Fires when an attachment inside a sent message is clicked. Use to open a lightbox / trigger a download.

events.name: pendingAttachmentClick
events.description: Fires when a pending (unsent) local-upload attachment is clicked in the input area — typically to preview before sending. `attachment` is a raw `File` object (no `url` yet — use `URL.createObjectURL(file)` for a preview).

events.name: close
events.description: Fires when the header close (×) button is clicked. Only visible when `headerShowCloseButton: true`. You're responsible for unmounting or hiding the chat in response.

localContext.description: Available to elements rendered inside the chat via binding only (the chat has no slots). Exposes the fully resolved conversation state.

currentUser.description: Participant with `isCurrentUser: true` — `{ id, name, avatar, location, status }`. Empty object if none is marked.

actions.label: Scroll to bottom
actions.description: Scroll the messages area to the latest message.

args.name: smooth
args.description: Use smooth animation (ignores `autoScrollBehavior` for this call).

variables.name: chatState
variables.description: Full computed state: `{ messages, conversation, currentUser, utils }`. Read-only — kept in sync with `messages` + `participants` + mapping formulas. Useful to read the resolved state from outside the chat.

specifications: MANDATORY SEED — never insert this element with empty `messages` and empty `participants` (it renders as a blank box with no header and looks broken). ALWAYS seed at least: 1 participant with `isCurrentUser: true`, 1 other participant, and 2-3 short demo messages with matching `senderId` ↔ `participants[].id`. Use light placeholder content — the user replaces it with their own bindings. This applies even when the user only says 'add a chat'.

MANDATORY SIZING — set `min-height: 500px` on the element style at insertion. The chat has internal flex regions (header / scrollable messages / input) and collapses to 0 height inside flex/grid parents without an explicit minimum. Without this rule, the element is invisible even when seeded correctly.

MANDATORY THEMING (when page is non-default) — the chat's internal sub-components have ISOLATED CSS and do NOT inherit page-level color / background / font. As soon as the page is anything other than the default light/white theme (dark, terminal, brutalist, editorial, branded palette…), you MUST override the FULL token bundle below — partial theming always produces broken visuals (white date-separator pill in a dark chat, soft-blue send button in a brutalist grid, grey bubbles on a colored page). Treat the bundle as all-or-nothing.
  TOKENS by zone (copy every one that applies):
  • Typography: `fontFamily`
  • Header: `headerBgColor`, `headerTextColor`, `headerBorder`, `groupChatAvatarColor`
  • Messages area: `messagesAreaBgColor`, `emptyMessageColor`
  • Date separator: `dateSeparatorTextColor`, `dateSeparatorLineColor`, `dateSeparatorBgColor`, `dateSeparatorBorderRadius` (the `Bg` one fixes the white pill)
  • Other participants' bubbles: `messageBgColor`, `messageTextColor`, `messageBorder`, `messageRadius`
  • Current user's bubbles: `ownMessageBgColor`, `ownMessageTextColor`, `ownMessageBorder`, `ownMessageRadius`
  • Input area: `inputBgColor`, `inputTextColor`, `inputPlaceholderColor`, `inputAreaBorder`, `textareaBorder`, `textareaBorderFocus`, `inputBorderRadius`
  • Send button: `sendButtonBgColor`, `sendButtonHoverBgColor`, `sendButtonBorder`, `sendButtonBorderRadius`, `sendButtonBoxShadow`, `sendIconColor`
  • Attachment button: `attachmentIconColor`
  Own-vs-other bubbles MUST be visually distinct (different bg color AND/OR different radius) — otherwise the conversation is unreadable.
DATA LINKING — every `messages[i].senderId` MUST match an `id` in `participants`. Without this link the chat cannot tell 'own' from 'other' messages.
Exactly ONE participant should carry `isCurrentUser: true`. If multiple do, behavior is undefined. If none do, every message renders as 'other'.

Send → persist → refresh flow — the chat does NOT auto-append sent messages. On `messageSent`: (1) accept the message from the event payload, (2) POST it to your backend, (3) push the confirmed message into the bound `messages` array. `messageReceived` then fires on other clients when their `messages` array gets the new item.
`messageReceived` is inferred from changes to the `messages` array (new entries whose `senderId !== currentUser.id`). It does NOT fire on the initial render, nor for messages the current user sent.
Keyboard in the input — `Enter` sends, `Shift+Enter` inserts a newline, `Escape` blurs the input.

Attachment lifecycle — user picks files → they become `pendingAttachments` (raw `File` objects, clickable via `pendingAttachmentClick`) → user sends → attachments arrive in `messageSent.payload.message.attachments` as `File[]` → you upload and replace with `{ id, name, type, size, url }` before storing in `messages`.

Group chat logic kicks in at `participants.length >= 2` (other than the current user). Avatar falls back to stacked initials; title falls back to `Group Chat (N participants)` unless `groupChatText` is set.
Locale / dates — auto-formatted in the configured `locale`: same-day messages show `timeFormat`, yesterday shows `yesterdayText`, older dates use the locale's long-date format.

DATE SEPARATOR — DOM-class shortcut: when the user mentions the class `ww-message-list__date-separator`, the centered date pill (Today / Yesterday / dates), the date separator stripe, or 'the white pill in the chat', this means set the 4 `dateSeparator*` properties (`dateSeparatorTextColor`, `dateSeparatorLineColor`, `dateSeparatorBgColor`, `dateSeparatorBorderRadius`). Custom CSS targeting `.ww-message-list__date-separator` will NOT work — the chat's internal stylesheet wins on specificity. The `Bg` token is the most common miss; without it the pill stays white over a themed page.

CONSTRAINTS (what NOT to do):
  • NO slots — the element renders its own header / messages / input. Do not try to add child elements.
  • DO NOT inject custom CSS via `classes` or inline `styles`. The listed style properties ARE the full theming surface. Repeated class-based attempts trigger infinite edit loops.
  • Mapping formulas (`mappingMessageId`, `mappingMessageText`, `mappingSenderId`, `mappingTimestamp`, `mappingAttachments`, `mappingAttachment*`, `mappingParticipant*`, `mappingIsCurrentUser`) are advanced — only use when the bound data shape differs from the default.
  • `close` is a UI-only signal — it does NOT unmount the chat. Wrap the element in a v-if or conditional container if you want hiding behavior.

examples.description: A 1-on-1 chat with two participants and a short history. Note how `senderId` in messages matches `id` in participants.
A group chat with 3 participants, custom header label, and close button enabled.
A disabled read-only chat (view-only, no input).

COMPLETE DARK TERMINAL PRESET — copy-paste this entire bundle as a reference for any dark theme. EVERY token listed below must be set when moving away from the default light theme, otherwise white/blue defaults leak through (date pill, send button shadow, bubble bg, ...).
examples.example: {"messages":[{"id":"m1","text":"ssh prod-01","senderId":"me","userName":"me","timestamp":"2026-04-23T10:00:00Z"},{"id":"m2","text":"Connected.","senderId":"sys","userName":"system","timestamp":"2026-04-23T10:00:01Z"}],"participants":[{"id":"me","name":"me","status":"online","isCurrentUser":true},{"id":"sys","name":"system","status":"online"}],"inputPlaceholder":"$ type a message…","sendIcon":"send","fontFamily":"JetBrains Mono, monospace","headerBgColor":"#0A0A0A","headerTextColor":"#00FF41","headerBorder":"1px solid rgba(0,255,65,0.3)","groupChatAvatarColor":"#00FF41","messagesAreaBgColor":"#0A0A0A","emptyMessageColor":"#4A9960","dateSeparatorTextColor":"#4A9960","dateSeparatorLineColor":"rgba(0,255,65,0.2)","dateSeparatorBgColor":"#0A0A0A","dateSeparatorBorderRadius":"2px","messageBgColor":"rgba(0,255,65,0.08)","messageTextColor":"#00FF41","messageBorder":"1px solid rgba(0,255,65,0.3)","messageRadius":"2px","ownMessageBgColor":"#00FF41","ownMessageTextColor":"#0A0A0A","ownMessageBorder":"1px solid #00FF41","ownMessageRadius":"2px","inputBgColor":"#0A0A0A","inputTextColor":"#00FF41","inputPlaceholderColor":"rgba(0,255,65,0.4)","inputAreaBorder":"1px solid rgba(0,255,65,0.3)","textareaBorder":"1px solid rgba(0,255,65,0.3)","textareaBorderFocus":"1px solid #00FF41","inputBorderRadius":"2px","sendButtonBgColor":"#00FF41","sendButtonHoverBgColor":"#4A9960","sendButtonBorder":"none","sendButtonBorderRadius":"2px","sendButtonBoxShadow":"none","sendIconColor":"#0A0A0A","attachmentIconColor":"#00FF41"}
